entry.title: Re: Health Monitoring | threshold | Response Time | task...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

please find a list of available Health Monitoring metrics for certain products here https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/operations/expert-portal/health-monitoring/health-mon-content.html

ABAP Metric "Response Time" contain data for all task types, including also dialog.

There you can set a threshold for the metric. Also you can configure an event to trigger email notifications for alerts.

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Project Kernseife - improving the measurement of Clean Core in ABAP
entry.content:
Hi @BjörnS !

Happy to share the content. We decided against an ABAP app for some reasons:

  • No direct integration possible: As our check relies on the standard check, we need to use functionality that is not released for ABAP Cloud. So a harmonized solution, where check and classification reside in one stack within BTP ABAP was not possible.
  • Side-by-Side: We needed to create the classification centrally, but several different landscapes (in different customer environments) needed to be considered. That is why we have a classic upload/download functionality, as it is the easiest way to spontaneously exchange data with different systems. In addition, we directly connect to e.g. the cloudification repository to check the current SAP release / nomination state.
  • There is no value add to choose ABAP then, as we would have needed an BTP ABAP environmnent internally just for this classification App. There, a simple CAP App was easier to achieve.
  • My colleague @DanielHuser was developing, he wanted to check in CAP.

Currently, this is not high on our list, so we will not "provide" this in the near future. But as it is open source, we would of course be happy to see someone creating a PR to add the RAP part! 🙂

entry.title: sign up for SAP Code Jam: Hands-on with the BTP CLI and APIs
entry.content:
Hello All,

We have great news for you!

In the global SAP community, there is a fine tradition for each edition of the SAP Inside Track conference to be preceded by a SAP Code Jam training session. This practice has also been preserved for the Wrocław event!

There will be SAP Code Jam: Hands-on with the BTP CLI and APIs led by the legendary DJ Adams!!!

 

When? June 7, 2025!

Where? 14G Fabryczna Street, Wroclaw, 2nd floor, room 220

About what?  Participants will learn about the ins and outs of the BTP CLI and APIs for SAP BTP, which are core services for SAP BTP. According to the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, these services enable” managing, building and extending the core capabilities of SAP BTP”.

What can I do? SIGN UP HERE

entry.title: Getting error in Eclipse ADT "Business Catalog is not supported in the selected project"
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am working on an SAP RAP (Restful ABAP Programming Model) application in Eclipse ADT. I need to schedule Background Job. While trying to create a Business Catalog using the context menu (New > Other Repository Object > Business Catalog), I get the following error:

"Business Catalog is not supported in the selected project"

I am not sure why this is happening, as I expected to add a catalog and create/assign Role to get Job template in Application Job screen. 

What I’ve Done:

  • Using Eclipse ADT (latest version) connected to an on-premise S/4HANA system.

  • I have created Application Job object and added sub object,  then created a class and created Application Job Catalog Entries and assigned class to it and then created Application Job Template (save and activated all).
  • I am unable to see custom template which I created in Application Job tile.
  • Upon further investigation, I got to know that we need to setup Authorizations for that I need to create Business catalog and Role for Authorization.
  • Right clicked on package and clicked on ABAP repository object but unable to find Business catalog. Then I selected my DEV system client from Eclipse, right clicked on it New ->other ->cloud Identity and access management ->Double clicked on Business Catalog, getting above error.

Question:

  • Why am I getting this error?

  • Is there a specific project type or system requirement for creating Business Catalogs in Eclipse ADT?

  • Do I need to configure something in the backend to enable this functionality?

Any help or guidance would be much appreciated!

Thanks,
Akbar Hussain

entry.title: Display approval workflow details in Manage Prices - Sales
entry.content:
Use

Approval requesters can monitor the approval process for prices in the Manage Prices – Sales app.

After you submit a price for approval, you can view its approval details in the corresponding condition record. You can track real-time progress and see assigned approvers and their comments in each approval step. You also have the option to see how the approval workflow is defined, ensuring clarity and transparency throughout the process.

This component enhances the functionality of the Object Page by embedding a workflow instance viewer, enabling users to seamlessly interact with workflow-related data directly within the application.

Users are able to check detail information of the workflow process:

  1. step name
  2. step title
  3. recipients
  4. step conditions
  5. assignment
  6. task title
  7. step process status (ready, cancel, etc.)

Prerequisite

Before we start using enhanced workflow functionalities in Manage Prices - Sales app, we have to know how to do the pre-configurations: Configuration for workflow in Prices, the contents in this blog will only show you the configured results and how the UI displays the workflow instance data.

The workflow section is dynamic visibility, only visible when below specific conditions are fulfilled:

  • the application is "Manage Prices - Sales"
  • the condition data is configured in a workflow process
  • the condition data is already in the workflow process (has approval request ID)

Display workflow details in Manage Prices - Sales app

Scenario1: 

Created a workflow process for only one of the approvers shall do action on the approval request, start condition is condition type is ZPR1

Other configuration parameters:

  • Step Name (Mandatory): Workflow Step for one of all recipients
  • Step Type (Only 1 selectable by default): Approve Sales Prices in Request
  • Priority: High
  • Assignment by: User
  • User: CB9980003361 -- Daisuke and CB9980003267 -- Richard
  • Step to be completed by: One of the recipients
  • Redefine Task Title(Optional): Task title for 1 recipient
  • step condition: condition type is ZPR1
  • other parts keep as default and no change 

Create a new condition record for ZPR1 and submit for approval. E.g. it is 0000092575

Any user could go to the object page of this data (0000092575), and since it has workflow process now, we could see a new section names "Approval Workflow". Display column there means:

  • Name = Workflow Step for one of all recipients (configured above), 
  • Type = Completion by single recipient,
  • Status = Ready
  • Processors = user(s) receive this request
  • Recipients =  user(s)/role(s) receive(s) this request

Click the item will go to its object page to see the detail information for this workflow process.

  • Title = Step Name (configured),
  • Step Type = Approve Sales Prices in Request (configured),
  • Priority = High (configured),
  • Last Action = the time when approver took action
  • Subject = Task title for 1 recipient (configured)

User can click "Show Definition Data" to see the brief definition for this workflow. The detail information will be expanded directly in the same page.

Once approver log on the Fiori Launch Pad, will see the price approve request in to-dos cards, or can open APP "My-Inbox" to see all approve request.

If approver clicks "Reject", a dialog for decision note (reject reason) will pop up, input decision note there and click "Submit".

Since ZPR1 is in workflow for 1 recipient, the process will be finished. If we back to the data object page, it shows status as "Rejected (with note)".

Click the item to enter the step detail page of this workflow process, we could see the "reject reason" in the column "Note".

 

Scenario2: 

Created a workflow process for all users shall do action on the approval request, start condition is condition type is ZNEO, other configured parameters:

  • Step Name (Mandatory): Workflow step for all of the recipients
  • Step Type (Only 1 selectable by default): Approve Sales Prices in Request
  • Priority: High
  • Assignment by: User
  • User: CB9980003361 -- Daisuke and CB9980003267 -- Richard
  • Step to be completed by: All of the recipients
  • Redefine Task Title(Optional): Task title for all recipient
  • step condition: condition type is ZNEO
  • other parts keep as default and no change 

Create a new condition record for ZNEO and submit for approval. E.g. it is 0000092578

Any user could go to the object page of this data (0000092578), and since it has workflow process now, we could see a new section names "Approval Workflow". Display columns there have the same meaning which be explained in above contents.

Once approver log on the Fiori Launch Pad, will see the price approve request in to-dos cards, or can open APP "My-Inbox" to see all approve request.

If approver clicks "Approve", a dialog for decision note (approve reason) will pop up, input decision note there and click "Submit".

Since ZNEO is in workflow for all recipients, the process will not be finished unless below condition be fulfilled:

  • all of the processors approve the workflow request
  • anyone of the processors rejects the workflow request

If we back to the data object page, it shows status with a process bar (e.g. 50% there with note).

Click the item there will go to its workflow step detail page and see the detail reason/time regarding user approved/rejected the workflow request there.

Unless all recipients approve the workflow request, the status will change to 100% and all approve reasons will be shown in the object page of the workflow step item of this data.

In addition, let's see a scenario for "completion by all recipients" but one of the processors rejects the workflow request. Same at the beginning, let's create a condition record:

If we go to its object page, we will see the status is 0%.

Status of all processors are "Ready".

Let's use processor "Daisuke" to reject the workflow with specified reason.

Back to the object page of this condition record, even only one user rejected the workflow, the whole workflow finished, and status changes to "Rejected" (with note).

We could check the reject reason there and also the status of other process(s) changed to "Cancelled".

Scenario3: 

Similar to Scenario1, difference is to use role as approver instead of user.

Scenario4: 

Similar to Scenario2, difference is to use role as approver instead of user.

Reference links 

Workflow in “Manage Prices – Sales” APP - SAP Community

Workflow Configuration for SAP S/4HANA Sales Price - SAP Community

Help Documentation of workflow

entry.title: Accounting posting should be split per item level at the time of posting in BSEG
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Hi Experts,

My client is asking us if it is possible to split account documents per item level at the time of posting in BSEG.

This question came up because of the condition contract rebate process. In standard, the rebate amounts are captured based on the material and posted to accounting as a cumulative amount.

Those amounts can be viewed in the entry view in the BSEG table.

EX:

GL account Discription Amount 
12345678Apple customer 66,91,741
98376904Gross Sales 3rd pty10,915,344-
67253987LT Price Red On Inv48,04,934
78308376Growth/scale Off CN5,11,924
29873648Acc. TTS/Cust. Act.-511,924
22734087Output tax account-318,654
23974576Gross Sales 3rd pty-527,040
12874084Oth Cust spc trms On2,64,363

GL 29873648 we are using to post the rebate amount -511,924 the a cumulative amount from the sales order.

Now client expectation is To be:

GL account Discription Amount 
12345678Apple customer 66,91,741

29873648Acc. TTS/Cust. Act.2,364-
29873648Acc. TTS/Cust. Act.2,364-
29873648Acc. TTS/Cust. Act.

4,728-

 

Please let me know is this split can be updated  in BSEG table and in SAP it can be achievable.

entry.title: AI-Assisted Support Query Resolution with Intent Induction and Matching
entry.content:
* Give this blog post a like / kudos to indicate your interest in seeing the AI feature described below in relevant upcoming SAP product releases, and feel free to leave a comment to share any further thoughts *

Problem Context

A number of ERP use cases are concerned with the resolution of support queries (e.g., reactive maintenance notifications in asset management, IT support tickets). In the absence of intelligent assistance, a support technician must manually go through each new support query and rely on existing business know-how to trigger downstream solution activities (e.g., carrying out a diagnostic test, ordering a replacement for a broken part). However, this fully manual approach to support query resolution is error-prone and hard to scale. Staffing support technicians is expensive, and these technicians may not always have the business know-how to accurately address the issues raised in the query.

An Illustrative Example: Resolving Maintenance Notifications

In reactive maintenance scenarios, a maintenance notification is created by a user to report a breakdown of an equipment at a manufacturing plant. The user enters data in the notification to describe the nature of the problem (e.g., equipment is affected, location, nature of the breakdown). For a given notification, a maintenance order can then be created to resolve the problem by carrying out a set of tasks (e.g., checking the equipment, removing the damaged piece, ordering a replacement). These steps can vary depending on the exact nature of the problem. AI can potentially assist the support technician in matching incoming support queries to appropriate downstream solution steps.

Solution Approach

In the following, a method of AI-assisted support query resolution using intent induction and matching is described, which can be implemented using SAP's suite of AI services.

Concept of Intents

In AI use cases where queries are matched with solutions, an intent reflects what the user wants to achieve by raising and resolving the query. The intent may be latent, i.e., not directly mentioned in the raw query. In general, a query may be associated with one or more intents, depending on how specific the query is. Moreover, one intent may be satisfied by more than one solution.

Example:

  • Query = "I have forgotten my password"
  • Intents relevant to the above query = ["Recover password", "Reset password"]
  • Solutions matching the above intents = ["Recover password by answering secret question", "Recover password by calling tech support", "Set new password"]

Workflows in Production

Large language models (LLMs), such as those available via SAP's Generative AI Hub, can parse the semantic meaning of data and be used to induce intents from queries.

Having a historical database of queries and their associated solutions is a key prerequisite to kickstart the AI-based method of intent induction using LLMs. To generate a corresponding intent database, an LLM is run over each query in the historical database to induce/generate one or more intents for them.

Now, whenever a new query comes in, an LLM is used to induce its intents (e.g., based on the query text). The intents of the new query are compared with the intents stored in the historical database to identify historical intents that are most similar. Solution steps related to these identified intents can then be provided as recommendations to the support technician. Finally, the intents and solutions of the new query are added to the historical database to guide future recommendations; to avoid excessive redundant data storage, this is only done if the information is not already captured in the database.

Accurate recommendations can improve the speed and accuracy of the support technician's work. In particular, rather than comparing the new query with the historical queries directly, the intent-based method checks similarity at the level of intents, which can improve scoring accuracy (e.g., queries that look superficially different may imply similar intents).

Avenues for Optimization

The intent-based approach can be optimized in several ways. First, the accuracy of solution recommendations can be improved over time by learning from the interactions with the support technicians during productive usage. In particular, whether the recommendations are accepted or rejected can provide a valuable signal for improving the relevance of future recommendations.

Second, components of the LLM-based intent induction architecture can be optimized, e.g., fine-tuning the prompt, using differentiated prompt templates per use case, and employing Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) to parse company-specific codes, shorthand and abbreviations. Translating all text data into a baseline English version can also simplify and improve the accuracy of intent induction and matching.

Third, as historical databases grow large, the AI-based matching can be optimized to reduce latency of recommendation retrieval. E.g., a cache of the most common intents can be separately maintained and checked first for matches before traversing the full database of historical intents. Furthermore, a map-reduce framework can be used to parallelize the search of the historical intent database.

Next Steps

Customers can consider a few different options to enhance their existing SAP workflows with an approach to AI-assisted support query resolution such as the one described above. E.g., customers may choose to implement this in-house or with the help of external partners/consultants, which can allow for customization but also incur related development and maintenance costs.

A better option may be to request AI-assisted support query resolution as a new feature in relevant upcoming SAP product releases. SAP can build the new feature in a way that ensures high product quality and reduces the total cost of ownership (TCO) for customers. Simply give this blog post a like/kudos to indicate your interest in seeing the AI feature described above in relevant upcoming SAP product releases, and feel free to leave a comment to share any further thoughts.

entry.title: TIVIT obtiene certificación SAP regional y reafirma su liderazgo en América Latina
entry.content:
En una entrevista con nuestro socio de larga trayectoria TIVIT, una empresa líder en tecnología en América Latina con presencia en varios países de la región, nos cuentan cómo apoyan la transformación digital de sus clientes y garantizan los más altos estándares en la operación de soluciones SAP.

También nos comparten detalles sobre su estructura regional y sus capacidades de servicio, que se ven reforzadas con la primera certificación regional de sus operaciones.

 

¿Para comenzar podría contarnos sobre su empresa y los servicios que ofrece?

TIVIT es una empresa líder en servicios de tecnología en América Latina, con presencia en varios países de la región. Se especializa en ofrecer soluciones integrales de TI que apoyan la transformación digital de sus clientes, combinando innovación, escalabilidad y eficiencia operativa.

Su portafolio de servicios abarca una amplia gama de soluciones diseñadas para acompañar a las empresas en su transformación digital. En el área de Cloud Solutions, TIVIT ofrece servicios de migración, gestión y operación de entornos en la nube pública, privada e híbrida, adaptándose a las necesidades específicas de cada cliente. A través de su unidad de Digital Business, desarrolla soluciones digitales innovadoras, impulsa la automatización de procesos, aplica analítica avanzada y mejora la experiencia del cliente.

En materia de Cybersecurity, proporciona servicios integrales de protección, monitoreo, gestión de identidades y respuesta ante incidentes, garantizando la seguridad de los entornos digitales. Su línea de Technology Platforms se centra en la administración y operación de infraestructura tecnológica, incluyendo centros de datos y redes, asegurando alta disponibilidad y eficiencia operativa.

Finalmente, en el ámbito de los SAP Services, TIVIT cuenta con una oferta especializada que incluye la operación, administración y soporte de soluciones SAP, con un enfoque particular en SAP S/4HANA y SAP HANA, consolidando así su compromiso con la excelencia y la innovación tecnológica.

Gracias a su enfoque regional y a sus certificaciones internacionales, TIVIT se posiciona como un socio estratégico para empresas que buscan eficiencia, confiabilidad y transformación tecnológica en entornos complejos.

 

¿Por qué decidió en 2008 certificar sus servicios como socio de operaciones de SAP?

La decisión respondió a una estrategia de negocio clara y alineada con la evolución del mercado. En ese momento, el número de clientes que adoptaban soluciones SAP crecía de forma sostenida, y como proveedores de servicios tecnológicos, entendimos que debíamos ser parte activa de ese ecosistema. Además, identificamos que nuestros clientes necesitaban concentrarse en el desarrollo de su negocio, delegando la operación técnica -especialmente los servicios SAP Basis- en un socio confiable. Para ofrecer ese respaldo, era fundamental contar con una certificación oficial que acreditara la calidad y el nivel técnico de nuestros servicios, validada por la misma SAP. En resumen, certificar nuestros servicios fue un paso esencial para construir una oferta sólida, competitiva y alineada con los más altos estándares del mercado.

 

TIVIT opera en varios países de América Latina, Ahora, por primera vez, TIVIT ha recibido la certificación regional para la operación de soluciones SAP S/4HANA y la operación de SAP HANA, para los países de Perú, Chile y Colombia. ¿Por qué han decidido optar por la certificación regional?

Efectivamente, TIVIT opera en múltiples países de América Latina, y avanzar hacia un modelo regional ha sido un paso natural en la evolución de nuestra gestión y atención de servicios TI. Esta regionalización nos ha llevado a rediseñar nuestro modelo de operación, con el objetivo de ofrecer una experiencia más integrada, eficiente y con estándares homogéneos de calidad. En ese contexto, optar por una certificación regional responde a la necesidad de validar, de manera objetiva y consistente, el nivel de excelencia con el que operamos las soluciones SAP en todos los países donde tenemos presencia. Es una forma de garantizar a nuestros clientes que, sin importar el país, recibirán un servicio de clase mundial.

 

¿Háblenos un poco más sobre sobre sus capacidades regionales para atender los servicios y operaciones SAP?

Nuestra presencia regional se ha construido a lo largo del tiempo, con una evolución constante en cada uno de los países donde operamos. Cada mercado ha desarrollado sus propias capacidades para atender y brindar servicios de forma autónoma, fortaleciendo así el músculo regional de TIVIT. En particular, los servicios SAP han sido un pilar estratégico en todas nuestras operaciones, lo que nos ha permitido consolidar un equipo técnico y profesional altamente calificado. Gracias a esta estructura regional y a la certificación obtenida, hoy contamos con una capacidad de atención que no solo destaca por su nivel técnico, sino también por su amplio alcance y cobertura en toda la región.

 

¿Qué impacto tendrá la certificación regional en la percepción de los clientes sobre TIVIT?

Si bien creemos que esta es una respuesta que idealmente deberían dar nuestros propios clientes, lo que algunos de ellos ya nos han compartido nos permite afirmar que la certificación regional incrementa significativamente su confianza en TIVIT. Contar con una acreditación que valide nuestro nivel y calidad técnica en la entrega de servicios -especialmente con alcance regional- transmite tranquilidad, refuerza la percepción de que estamos a la vanguardia del sector y consolida nuestra capacidad de ofrecer soluciones consistentes y de alto nivel desde cualquiera de los países en los que operamos.

 

La información sobre el programa de certificación para socios operativos y una visión general de todos los socios certificados se puede encontrar en SAP Operations Partner Guide.

entry.title: Moving Q-stock between Plants
entry.content:
Hi,

I need the following process:

1. Post Quality stock from Production order on Plant A

2. Move Quality stock from Plant A to Plant B

3. Record inspection results and Usage decision for inspection lot, so that to get Unrestricted stock on Plant B

---------------------------------

To move Q-stock between Plants I used: Purchase order (PUB stock transfer) and outbound delivery.

Preliminary I did the following configuration to allow Purchase order picking Q-stock:

Service Parts Management (SPM) > Transfer and Inventory Management (SPM) > Activate Source Stock Type Determination

------------------------------

Which result I have:

1. Q-stock was successfully picked from Plant A for Stock transfer order with Mvmnt 641

2. Posted on Plant B. The problem that it was posted as "Unrestricted"

So the question is: how to pick Q-stock from "Plant A" and posted the same Q-stock on "Plant B"

PS. Stock transfer Mvmnt 301 does not allow picking "Q-stock"

entry.title: How can we update orders via REST api?
entry.content:
SAP DM offers a REST api for many purposes. We're building a connection between (one of) our ERP system and SAP DM. Production orders are released in the ERP and pushed to SAP DM via Mulesoft. We are looking for 2 other options:

  1. Delete an order
  2. Update an order

Within REST we want to use CRUD for all api's. But SAP DM does not offer these options. 

For the delete we can use the discard option. This makes an order in SAP DM not useable anymore.

For an update its harder. With the absence of an update api we think about deleting an order and create it again with new values. The problem is, since deleting is not actually deleting but discarding, we're not able to create a new order with the same order number.  It is important to work with the same order numbers because in the feedback loop, when doing production yield and scrap, we need to notify on which order number this is done.

entry.title: SAP HANA Log Restore Using NetBackup on a Different System
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I have a HANA database (log mode: normal) that is being backed up via NetBackup — both full data backups and log backups.

Now I’m restoring the backups to a different HANA system.

Here are my two scenarios:

Scenario 1 – Restore Only Logs After Full Backup Already Restored:

I have already restored the full backup from 9 PM.

Now I want to restore only the log backups, on top of that, without re-restoring the full backup.

But when I try to do that, it starts restoring the full backup again.

→ How can I restore only the logs after full backup is already restored?

Scenario 2 – Restore Logs Up to a Specific Time:

I want to restore logs only up to two hours after the full backup (i.e., until 11 PM).

How can I control the log restore process to stop at a specific point in time?

Any help or step-by-step guidance (using HANA Studio, or Backint) would be really appreciated.

Thanks a lot!

entry.title: Changes in SAP GUI 800 Patch 5+ Affecting RPA Automation (Windows)
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I’m encountering automation failures in SAP GUI scripting starting from Patch 5 (800.5.x) onward, while the same scripts work perfectly in Patch 4 (800.4.x) and earlier versions.

The RPA is is working for all versions including and below SAP GUI 800 Patch 4 but starts failing from version 800 Patch 5.

Issue Description

  • SAP GUI Version:
    • Working: SAP GUI 800 Patch 4 (800.4.x)
    • Failing: SAP GUI 800 Patch 5+ (800.5.x and above)
  • Error Symptoms:
    • Scripts fail to interact with TreeItem (shown as SAP's Advanced Treelist in inspect.exe)
    • UI element selectors (e.g., [CLASS:TreeItem; NAME:2:0; CELL:0,0]) no longer work

Questions for the Community

  1. Are there documented changes in SAP GUI 800 Patch 5+ that affect UI automation?

    • Were accessibility/COM interface modifications introduced?
    • Is there a new recommended way to interact with TreeList/Grid controls?

  2. Has SAP introduced new security or scripting restrictions?

    • Does Patch 5+ enforce stricter checks on UI automation?

  3. Workarounds or Fixes?

    • Has anyone successfully adapted RPA scripts for Patch 5+?

  4. Official SAP Guidance?

    • Are there SAP Notes or KB articles addressing this?

Additional Details

  • Inspect.exe Comparison:
    • Patch 4 vs. Patch 5 shows no structural UI changes.

entry.title: Are OData V2 Services Available in OData V4 in SAP, and Which Ones?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I hope you’re all doing well! I’m currently working on a project involving SAP Gateway and OData services, and I have a question regarding the availability of OData services across different versions.
We are using several OData V2 services in our current SAP system (SAP S/4HANA 2022), and we are planning to transition to OData V4 to leverage its advanced features (e.g., improved metadata, batch processing, and better support for modern APIs). However, I’m unsure whether the OData V2 services we rely on are also available in OData V4, or if we’ll need to redevelop or adjust our integrations.
Here’s what I’d like to know:
1- Are OData V2 services generally available in OData V4 in SAP systems? If not, how can we identify which ones have equivalent V4 versions?
2 - Specifically, we are using the following OData V2 services:

  • API_PRODUCT_SRV – Using for Material Master as a Product
  • API_EQUIPMENT – Using for Equipment Master
  • API_FUNCTIONALLOCATION – Using Functional location
  • API_MAINTENANCEBOM -  Using for Material BOM and Equipment BOM
  • API_BILL_OF_MATERIAL_SRV -  Using for Material BOM
  • API_MAINTENANCEPLAN -  Using for Maintenance Item
  • API_MAINTENANCEPLAN -  Using for Maintenance Plan
  • API_BUSINESS_PARTNER -  Using for Business Partne(Vendor/Customer)
  • API_CLFN_CLASS_SRV – Using for Class
  • API_CLFN_CHARACTERISTIC_SRV – Using for Characteristics
  • API_MAINTNOTIFICATION – Using for Maintenance Notification
  • API_MAINTENANCEORDER -  Using for Maintenance Order

    Can anyone confirm if these services (or their equivalents) are available in OData V4? If yes, what are their technical names in V4, and are there any significant differences in their structure or functionality?

        Is there a systematic way (e.g., a report, transaction, or SAP documentation) to check which OData V2             services have been migrated or reimplemented in ODataV4?

Looking forward to your expertise and guidance. Thank you in advance for your help!

Best regards,

entry.title: Exclude blocked (or marked for deletion) customers from appearing in future transactions
entry.content:
Dears,
The case is that I have a customer who has a lot of Ship-to assigned, and I need to block/delete a few of them so they are not visible when processing (new) Sales orders.

I blocked them and marked them for deletion (using XD05 and XD06 for all areas, etc.), but when creating a Sales order, I still see them in the 'Partner selection' pop-up when choosing the Ship-to address for that customer. The correct error message appears when I select one of them ("There is an order block for customer..."), but they are still visible on the list, which causes confusion for other users.

How can I process these customers/Ship-to to exclude them from being visible in future sales documents?
(SAP ECC 6.0 Enh Pack 5)

P.S. I know there is an available transaction to completely remove customers (OBR2), but this should be a last resort, used only if there are no other options and no open transactions.

Reagrds
Krzysztof

entry.title: Re: Exclude blocked (or marked for deletion) customers from appearing in future transactions
entry.content: Dear Krzysztof, Blocking or marking Ship-to customers for deletion (XD05/XD06) in SAP ECC prevents processing, but it doesn't hide them from the 'Partner selection' pop-up in sales orders. To achieve this, your most robust solution is to implement a User Exit or Business Add-In (BAdI), such as within MV45AFZZ, to add custom ABAP code that filters the F4 help results and excludes these blocked or deleted Ship-to partners from the list presented to users.

entry.title: TIVIT’s Strategic Approach to Driving Cloud Migration and Digital Transformation
entry.content:
In this interview, our long-standing partner TIVIT outlines its structured approach to digital transformation, driven by a dedicated Line of Business established in Brazil. The company shares insights into its experience as a certified operations partner, including its recent achievement in SAP BTP operations. The conversation also touches on how TIVIT supports clients on their journey to SAP S/4HANA cloud, aligning with RISE with SAP and GROW with SAP. Readers will gain a clear view of TIVIT’s technical capabilities and strategic direction in enabling scalable and secure digital transformation across Latin America.

 

To begin, could you tell us about TIVIT and the range of services you offer to your clients in the region?

TIVIT is a Brazilian multinational company that connects technology for a better world. With over 25 years of experience and operations in ten Latin American countries, it specializes in creating transformative digital projects. Through its constantly evolving business lines, the company supports organizations in ensuring sustainability and scalability with cloud solutions (public, hybrid, and private), enables secure environments with cybersecurity resources, develops technologies focused on Artificial Intelligence, and offers services in automation and SaaS solutions. The digital transformation driven by TIVIT impacts millions of people daily. 

 

Since TIVIT's first certification, the company has expanded the certification scope—re-certifying its initial certification several times and adding new ones. Most recently, TIVIT was certified for SAP BTP operations. What was the deciding factor behind this decision?

SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) is a strategic pillar for digital transformation and operational efficiency, and it also plays a key role in the Clean Core strategy, ensuring a more agile, flexible, and standardized ERP. To keep up with market evolution, TIVIT has invested in creating a dedicated and highly qualified team, with professionals certified in SAP BTP. Being a certified operations partner for SAP BTP reinforces our commitment to innovation, operational excellence, and the delivery of high-value solutions to our clients, ensuring governance over our processes.

 

SAP continues to align its strategy with the cloud and encourages partners to play an active role in supporting customers throughout their migration journey. How do you plan to support your customers in their ongoing digital transformation? 

TIVIT is committed to expanding its expertise in SAP cloud solutions and aims to increase the number of certifications in this area by 40% in 2025. This growth reflects our strategic positioning as a business partner, actively supporting migrations to SAP S/4HANA Cloud in Brazil, through the Rise with SAP and Grow with SAP offerings.

 

Can you elaborate on TIVIT’s approach to Cloud migration support and what sets your digital transformation strategy apart for clients?

Since the creation of a dedicated Line of Business (LoB) for SAP offerings in Brazil in 2022, TIVIT has established itself as a strategic partner to drive digital transformation. With a team of over 450 specialized professionals, we work on migration projects and comprehensive management of SAP environments, offering a 360-degree approach that encompasses infrastructure, functional support, user and consumption control, cybersecurity, process consulting, and modernization. Our expertise delivers efficiency, innovation, and security, enabling our clients to maximize value and reduce costs through SAP solutions.

 

 

Information on the certification program for operations partners and an overview of all certified partners can be found in the SAP Operations Partner Guide.

Visit OPC at SAP.com

entry.title: Re: Error occurring while accessing SAP BTP Cloud trial a...

entry.title: Re: How can we mass update SAPAPO Product group and PRODUCT Group type in a S4 System?
entry.content:
In MASSD/MASS transaction, you can update the GROUPVALUE field of table /SAPAPO/MATGROUP but not the field GROUPTYPE.
This limitation occurs because GROUPTYPE is a key field of table /SAPAPO/MATGROUP. Key fields cannot be changed through mass transactions. Key fields can only be deleted and created - not changed.

A recent SAP Note has been released that allows the creation of new entries in /SAPAPO/MATGROUP in transaction code MASS:
3599807 - Enable mass creation of product group assignments
This note enables the official means of creating new entries in transaction MASS, including creation with reference.

For creation, you can use a selection criterion with 'GROUPVALUE' set to fixed value ' ' (blank character). The selection result will be the list of missing entries which fit the other selection criteria. For deletion, you can save a product group entry with an initial GROUPVALUE.

entry.title: Re: How can we mass update SAPAPO Product group and PRODU...
entry.content:
In MASSD/MASS transaction, you can update the GROUPVALUE field of table /SAPAPO/MATGROUP but not the field GROUPTYPE.
This limitation occurs because GROUPTYPE is a key field of table /SAPAPO/MATGROUP. Key fields cannot be changed through mass transactions.
Key fields can only be deleted and created - not changed.

A recent SAP Note has been released that allows the creation of new entries in /SAPAPO/MATGROUP in transaction code MASS:
3599807 - Enable mass creation of product group assignments

This note enables the official means of creating new entries in transaction MASS, including creation with reference. For creation, you can use a selection criterion with 'GROUPVALUE' set to fixed value ' ' (blank character). The selection result will be the list of missing entries which fit the other selection criteria. For deletion, you can save a product group entry with an initial GROUPVALUE.

entry.title: Your Network Resilience & Redundancy with SAP ECS: A technical take
entry.content:
Who is this for?

For network architects wrangling with ECS on connectivity design and decisions. Background networking knowledge is essential. I won’t discuss more on the connectivity concepts but will detail elements that you can leverage to enhance the resilience & redundancy of your network connectivity to ECS

Considerations with SAP ECS

ECS recommends that customers treat the ECS network, where SAP systems reside, as a logical extension of their own network infrastructure. As a result of this (as of this writing), ECS does not natively offer advanced routing techniques, tunnel monitoring tools, or automated tunnel failover mechanisms. Instead, the responsibility for designing and implementing the desired redundancy and resiliency in connectivity rests with you, the customer. This entails leveraging additional (third-party) tools and appropriately configuring your on-premises/hyperscaler network equipment in collaboration with ECS to achieve the required level of network resilience.

Deciding on VPN Connection? Proceed with Caution

In the rush to get projects off the ground, many organizations establish a basic VPN tunnel to connect their on-premises infrastructure to the SAP RISE environment, often relying on a single tunnel. This "quick fix" approach, while expedient, can introduce several challenges:

  • Latency and Performance Issues: VPNs, operating over the public internet, are susceptible to congestion and unpredictable bandwidth, leading to inconsistent response times.
  • Scalability Concerns: As your organization grows, increasing VPN bandwidth to meet demands can be problematic.
  • High Availability Limitations: Achieving high availability with a single VPN tunnel is inherently challenging.

These issues are particularly pronounced during brownfield migration projects, where reliable and high-performance connectivity is vital for efficient data migration to ECS.

Implementing Redundant VPN Tunnels

To bolster your network's resilience, consider the following strategies:

  • Multiple VPN Tunnels Over a Single ISP: Establishing multiple VPN tunnels between the same endpoints—your site and ECS—over a single ISP link can provide parallel paths for data transmission. While this setup offers some redundancy, it remains vulnerable to ISP-level outages.
  • Multiple VPN Tunnels Over Redundant ISPs: For greater resilience, set up VPN tunnels over multiple ISPs. This approach eliminates reliance on a single ISP, creating a fully redundant network. Each ISP provides independent paths for VPN connectivity, enhancing fault tolerance.

Optimizing Routing for Resilience

Effective routing is key to maximizing the benefits of your redundant VPN setup: For routing, using protocols like BGP dynamically adjusts routing tables based on network conditions, ensuring efficient use of available tunnels. For policy-based routing, add static route and weight the backup route. Honestly, though, you'll save yourself a world of headaches by leaning into dynamic routing with BGP

If you’re choosing to have active-active VPN tunnel, then each of those routes can be assigned different costs or priority, and the edge device will handle the traffic. It can be round-robin, load balancing or simple failover although the suggested one is simple failover.

Note traffic sent through one tunnel might return via another, leading to asymmetric routing. This can cause problems, especially with stateful firewalls that expect return traffic on the same interface.

By using route preference techniques such as AS path prepending can influence the preferred path for incoming traffic. For instance, using AS path prepending to make one tunnel less preferred, guiding traffic through the desired path. Also, ECMP (Equal-Cost Multi-Path) allows for load balancing across multiple paths of equal cost, distributing traffic evenly and reducing the risk of asymmetry.

Continuously monitoring the performance of each VPN tunnel and adjusting routing preferences based on metrics like latency, jitter, and packet loss might be required. This proactive approach ensures that traffic uses the most efficient and stable paths.

And you don't have to go it alone! There are some excellent tools out there to help you out. You can use any tools like Palo Alto Networks – Tunnel Monitoring where Palo Alto firewalls offer built-in tunnel monitoring features like ICMP probing, Dead Peer Detection (DPD) and also Automatic failover. Similarly, ManageEngine OpManager provides Real-Time Monitoring with alerts and notifications. Also, SolarWinds Network Performance Monitor (NPM) provides Site-to-Site VPN Monitoring.

Other hyperscaler connectivity

When it comes to hyperscaler connectivity, that's a whole different ballgame! Think of it less about ECS and more about the specific cloud provider's playbook. For the nitty-gritty details, your best bet is to dive into hyperscaler documentation and get that done by ECS.

AWS Direct Connect

Refer to this to know more on AWS Direct Connect Resiliency. Decide on the architecture and ask ECS to configure the resiliency model when they order the Direct Connect Connection. Depending on if you’re working with AWS Direct Connect Partner, you might want to involve them as well in the discussions.

Azure Express Route

For Express Route redundancy, refer Express Route Private Peering. With the decided architecture you might want to work with ECS to ensure advertised routes over ExpressRoute circuit is visible on ECS side. For weighted path, you’ll have to work with ECS to get it configured, and for AS path prepending, on the less preferred tunnel, configure a route-map to prepend your AS number multiple times.

Further Tips

  • Firewall Configuration: Ensure that your firewalls are configured to allow traffic from multiple interfaces (ports & protocols)
  • NAT Traversal: Be cautious with NAT configurations, as they can complicate routing and lead to asymmetry. ECS might push you back if you’ve NAT configurations.
  • Resiliency Testing: Regularly test failover scenarios to ensure that redundant paths function correctly without introducing asymmetry. Talk to your ECS representatives to plan this
  • Documentation: Maintain thorough documentation of your network configuration and work with ECS representative to track and review modifications that could affect routing.

 

 

Got other connectivity curiosities you'd like me to poke into for SAP ECS? Drop a comment below—I'm always happy to write another blog post about it! If this post sparked more questions than answers (or just got your tech gears grinding), don't hesitate to reach out! I'm happy to chat more about ECS and dive deeper into any technical topics you have in mind

entry.title: Unlocking Trade Spend Control Efficiency with Funds Management in SAP Revenue Growth Management
entry.content:
1. An Introduction to Funds Management  

In the fast-paced world of consumer-packaged goods (CPG), trade promotions are essential for boosting sales and capturing market share. Yet, controlling promotion spend often feels like navigating a maze of inefficiencies. Manual processes for setting up funds and budgets eat up valuable time and introduce errors. Overly complicated fund structures bog down teams with details needed for financial compliance, yet irrelevant to budget allocation and sales target achievement, pulling focus away from achieving sales targets. And without real-time visibility into budget usage, decision-makers are left guessing, unable to act quickly or confidently. These hurdles don’t just slow things down—they limit a company’s ability to adapt and thrive in a competitive market.  

 

2. Introducing SAP Revenue Growth Management 

SAP Revenue Growth Management’s funds management transforms this challenge. As a cloud-native consumer products industry solution for trade promotion planning, management, and execution, SAP Revenue Growth Management serves as the strategic replacement for SAP Trade Management for trade promotion planning and execution in consumer products. 

SAP Revenue Growth Management has an active roadmap and delivers enhancements and new features every month. One of the recent feature additions is funds management. Tailored for the CPG industry, this new feature transforms how trade spend budgets are managed, from setup to reservation and oversight. Here’s how it makes a difference. 

 

3. Funds Management in Action: Three Keys to Trade Spend Control Efficiency 

3.1 Efficient Fund and Budget Creation: Speed and Accuracy 

Traditional trade promotion systems often force teams into a slow, error-prone slog: creating funds and assigning budgets as separate, manual tasks. For a CPG company juggling hundreds or even tens of thousands of customer-product combinations, this could stretch into days of repetitive work. The funds management in SAP Revenue Growth Management flips that script with two powerful capabilities. 

  • Bulk-Create Funds with Budgets Using Customer and Product Hierarchies

Imagine a sales finance manager gearing up for the new year. In legacy systems, they’d painstakingly set up each fund one by one. With Funds management, they can generate thousands of funds and assign budget for each in one go, pulling directly from master data such as existing customer and product hierarchies. What once took days now takes minutes—say, creating budgets for every product (like soda or snacks) in every store or chain they partner with—in a single click. This speed doesn’t just save time; it cuts down on human error, ensuring funds are set up accurately from the start. 

  • Combine Fund Creation and Budget Assignment via Excel Upload

Funds can be created via master data or through spreadsheet upload. The spreadsheet import method allows users to set up funds and assign fixed budgets in a single step. Users download a template, which can include pre-filled customer/product data, and input fund details and budget figures side by side. When the updated template is uploaded, SAP Revenue Growth Management creates funds and assigns budgets simultaneously. This streamlines processes and frees sales finance managers to prioritize planning and strategy. 

 

3.2 Simplified Fund Structures: Clarity and Flexibility 

Complicated fund structures can turn trade promotion management into a headache, especially for Key Account Managers (KAMs) who focus on driving sales. When fund structures are aligned directly with account plans, it becomes easier for KAMs to understand their budgets and realize sales targets, rather than getting lost in complex setups. SAP Revenue Growth Management’s funds management simplifies this by putting usability first. 

  • Flexibility in Fund Dimension Setup

CPG companies can decide and align the fund dimensions based on KAMs’ planning practice with their account plans. Since account plan targets relate to the customer or customer plus product level, fund dimensions could be set up accordingly to allocate budgets at the right level to match the sales target. In particular, the legacy “fund type” dimension is kept optional but remains available. This feature breaks down budgets by spend type/payment type, which is more relevant for financial reporting and payment processing. While KAMs typically don't need to manage these details, it allows them to distribute funding even down to different types of spend. 

  • Consolidate Funds for Multi-Product Account Plans

Managing a fund for every single product can quickly spiral into chaos. Funds management lets users bundle multiple products—like beverage and food—into one fund. Picture a KAM overseeing an account plan for both beverage and food. Instead of tracking two separate funds, they manage one, aligned neatly with their account plan. This slashes the number of funds to oversee and equips KAMs with clear visibility into their core tasks, simplifying performance tracking during and after promotions.  

By minimizing complexity, funds management bridges the gap between sales and finance, enabling KAMS to focus on growing the business while giving both teams a clearer, shared view of the budget. 

 

3.3 Automated Fund Determination: Seamless Budget Control 

Sales finance managers need to apply budget control policies during the planning process, while KAMs need to know which fund can be used when working on account plans and promotions. Automated fund determination enables seamless budget control by finding eligible funds automatically during account plan and promotion creation and tracking budget reservation. 

  • Determined Funds at Account Plan

When a KAM creates an account plan, the eligible fund for the plan is automatically determined and displayed. KAMs can easily know the available budget to achieve sales targets. 

  • Budget Control at Promotion

When a KAM creates a promotion, the eligible fund for this promotion is automatically determined and displayed. The remaining budget is automatically checked. Based on CPG companies' budget control policies, promotions can still be saved even if they result in over consumption of the available fund (a soft control policy). KAMs are alerted to any potential overspending of the funds available.  This control policy will be configurable in the future to support hard control policies that block promotions exceeding available funding sources. 

 

3.4 Real-Time KPIs and Audit-Ready Transparency: Visibility and Control 

Reactive fund monitoring is outdated. Previously, KAMs and sales finance managers needed to manually search, track, and combine fund budgets and struggled to check budget reservation. Funds management in SAP Revenue Growth Management provides the visibility and precision needed to stay ahead. 

  • Fund Dashboard for the Full Picture

If a KAM has created funds for 2025 and 2026, they'll get two real-time summary cards on the dashboard—one for each year. Each card displays key figures such as the total number of funds, number of over-consumed funds, total budget, available and reserved budgets for quick actions.   

  • Detailed Drill-Down Navigation

Users can dive from high-level figures into specific budget and consumption posting documents, along with promotion details. For example, when KAMs need to revise a budget, they can adjust it directly on the fund's detail screen. If sales finance managers notice a fund edging close to its limit, a few clicks later, they can review detailed documents to understand which promotion activities are reserving and consuming funds, and tweak plans on the fly, dodging overspend and supporting revenue growth. It’s like having a live pulse on every dollar, keeping spending in check without the guesswork.  

 

4. Co-Innovation: Turn Funds into a Growth Engine 

Funds management in SAP Revenue Growth Management is more than a tool—it’s a foundation for smarter promotions, error-free processes, and revenue-driven trade strategies. 

If you’re struggling with: 

  • Manual processes delaying fund setup 
  • Complex fund structures 
  • Applying budget control policies during account and promotion creation 
  • Limited visibility into trade spend control and budget consumption 

Join us in co-innovating new features already on our roadmap to revolutionize sales finance: 

  • Dynamic / rate-based budget allocation based on actual sales trends for responsive planning. 
  • Advanced automation for fund and budget creation, seamlessly integrated with planning tools such as SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC). 
  • Intelligent funds creation, orchestrating the entire process to enhance efficiency and reduce manual effort. 

Whether you’re a CPG brand refining promotion strategy, or a sales finance manager demanding smarter controls, your input could shape the future of SAP Revenue Growth Management. Email RGM_Engage@sap.com to get started. Together, we’ll unlock new levels of efficiency and growth. 

 

More Information

Funds Document on SAP Help Portal

Topic Page for SAP Revenue Growth Management

  • Decoding Promotional Success: Embedded Analytics in SAP Revenue Growth Management
  • Introducing Calendar View in SAP Revenue Growth Management: A Visual Leap for Strategic Planning

No, transformation flow is a separate type. See documentation here: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/c8a54ee704e94e15926551293243fd1d/f7161e6c20204672ac4a6d90c81762e4.html

entry.title: Re: Is it Possible to Edit the Email Body of a BTM Task S...
entry.content:
Currently there is no way to edit the subject or body of the business task message. 

As an alternative, you may use a workflow rule (if applicable in your business scenario) to send notification e-mails. This way it is possible to control the e-mail sent by the rule.

You can check the documentation for Workflow Rules:

Workflow Rule Document for Business ByDesign - Workflow Rules Quick Guide

entry.title: Major bug in SAP. We do not need SP Key rather we should have source plant and destination field
entry.content: Currently SP Key field in MRP 2 in MM03 is created unnecessarily since we do not have any field called source plant and destination plant in S4 Hana. There are lot of problems for creation of SP key which is unique to company based on source plant and destination plant. For every new plant transfer, new SP key needs to be created and maintained. SAP has created this problem. Instead for every material, source plant and destination plant fields should have been given in MRP 2 view in MM03.

entry.title: Re: How to create custom API for Talent Intelligence Hub data to integrate with downstream system(s)
entry.content:
Hi @AnnaChobanska1 - Thank you for your response. CPI is Cloud Platform Integration, yes customers need to have it separately. Integration center will also work, but there are few challenges with it, like when you want to only update few specific skills/other attributes whose data got updated in last 24hrs(as you don't want to update all the skills in custom MDF daily!), that can't be achieved as TIH related options in Integration Center doesn't allow any kind of filters. Also by adding skills/other attributes daily in the MDF would create multiple records for same skills/other attributes daily as we need to maintain the effective date of change of skill/other attributes in the MDF for downstream systems. Hence we had to move towards CPI for putting any such rules, criteria while updating the custom MDF. Hope this helps!

Thanks

Ujjal

entry.title: Re: How to delete FI document generated from Goods Receip...
entry.content: The expense account X in SAP is actually the same as the expense account A in the legacy system. Since this expense account has already been posted in SAP (due to auto-posting from GR/SES), migrating the full transaction data—including postings to this same expense account—from the legacy system will distort the Trial Balance. The key concern from the accounting team is that all balances per G/L account must remain accurate and consistent. They require that no postings to G/L accounts (especially expense accounts) exist in SAP prior to the official FICO go-live.
